zotero.abstractNote: Deep neural networks are vulnerable to adversarial examples, which are inputs with small, human-imperceptible perturbations that cause the model to make incorrect predictions. Existing defenses against adversarial examples mainly focus on robust optimization, which often leads to a trade-off between standard accuracy and adversarial robustness. In this paper, we investigate the adversarial vulnerability of deep neural networks from a causal perspective. We show that adversarial examples are often caused by the model's reliance on non-robust features, which are statistically correlated with the label but are not causally related to it. We propose a causal framework for adversarial robustness, which aims to learn a model that relies on causal features. We further propose a method, called CausalAdv, to learn such a model. Experimental results on several benchmark datasets demonstrate that our method achieves state-of-the-art adversarial robustness while maintaining high standard accuracy.
